title: Speed
description: How fast to drive — and why the posted number is only a maximum.
_note: School-zone, residential, and interstate speed numbers vary by posting; verify any specific limit against the current Kentucky Driver Manual before adding number-specific questions.

scenarios.concept: Speed limit is a maximum, not a target
scenarios.prompt: The sign says 55, but it is raining hard and difficult to see. How fast should you drive?

choices.label: Slower than 55 — only as fast as is safe
Exactly 55, because that is the limit
Faster, to get out of the rain sooner
Stop in your lane until the rain ends

feedback.correct: Yes — the number is the most you may go in good conditions. In rain, snow, or fog you must slow down.
feedback.exact: The limit is a maximum for good weather. In heavy rain you must go slower. Try again.
feedback.faster: Never go faster than is safe — and never over the limit. Slow down in rain. Try again.
feedback.stop: Don't stop in a travel lane. Slow down and keep moving carefully. Try again.
feedback.default: Drive slower than the posted limit when the weather is bad. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You are driving through a school zone and the yellow lights are flashing. What should you do?

choices.label: Slow to the lower school-zone speed and watch for children
Speed up to clear the zone quickly
Honk so children move out of the way

feedback.correct: Yes — flashing lights mean slow to the posted school-zone speed and watch closely for kids.
feedback.normal: Flashing lights mean a slower limit is in effect. Slow down. Try again.
feedback.fast: Never speed up in a school zone. Slow down and watch for children. Try again.
feedback.honk: Honking won't keep kids safe — slow to the school-zone speed. Try again.
feedback.default: Slow to the posted school-zone speed when the lights flash. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Traffic around you is moving at the speed limit. Is it safe to drive much slower than everyone else?

choices.label: No — going much too slow can block traffic and cause crashes
It is fine to slow to a stop if you are unsure

feedback.correct: Right — driving far below the flow of traffic is dangerous. Keep up with traffic, up to the limit.
feedback.yes: Too slow can be as dangerous as too fast — it makes others brake and swerve. Try again.
feedback.stop: Never stop in a travel lane. Keep up with the flow of traffic. Try again.
feedback.default: Going much slower than traffic is dangerous. Keep up with the flow, up to the limit. Try again.
